title: Pentameris provincialis
taxonomy: Pentameris provincialis is classified as a species within the genus Pentameris, which is a part of the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. The Poaceae family is further grouped into the order Poales. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. The order Poales is a part of the clade commelinids, which also includes other flowering plant orders such as the Zingiberales and the Arecales. The clade commelinids is a subgroup within the larger clade angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants.

intro: Pentameris provincialis, also known as Bristle Bent Grass, is a species of grass native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a potential remedy for coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Pentameris provincialis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Pentameris provincialis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support respiratory health and soothe bronchial irritation.
